content: Can you help me write a Python function to parse JWT tokens? I need it for the auth module.
Sure, I'll write a JWT parser for the auth module. Let me create that.

name: write_file

content: I've created the JWT utilities file at auth/jwt_utils.py. It includes token creation, verification, and user ID extraction.
Now I need you to read the existing auth config to understand what's already there.

name: read_file
content:
# Auth configuration
AUTH_PROVIDER = 'internal'
SESSION_DURATION = 86400  # 24 hours in seconds
REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL = 2592000  # 30 days
ALLOWED_ORIGINS = ['https://app.example.com', 'https://staging.example.com']
RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE = 60
MFA_REQUIRED = True

# Database
DB_POOL_SIZE = 10
DB_MAX_OVERFLOW = 20

The existing config shows: AUTH_PROVIDER is 'internal', sessions last 24 hours, refresh tokens last 30 days, MFA is required, and rate limiting is 60 requests/minute. I'll align the JWT utils with these settings.
Good. Also I want to enforce that all JWT tokens for this project use RS256 (asymmetric) not HS256. It's more secure for production.
Switching to RS256 is the right call for production — asymmetric signing means the public key can be distributed for verification without exposing the private key. Let me update the implementation.

name: edit_file
content: File edited successfully. Changed ALGORITHM from 'HS256' to 'RS256' in auth/jwt_utils.py
Run the tests to make sure nothing broke.

name: exec
content:
tests/test_auth.py::test_create_token PASSED
tests/test_auth.py::test_verify_token PASSED
tests/test_auth.py::test_expired_token PASSED
tests/test_auth.py::test_invalid_token PASSED
tests/test_auth.py::test_get_user_id PASSED

5 passed in 0.43s
All 5 auth tests pass. The JWT utilities are working correctly with RS256.
Perfect. I also want to document that this project uses JWT for stateless authentication — no server-side sessions.
Noted. The auth architecture is stateless JWT — tokens are verified by signature without server-side session storage. This is the intended design.
